intro: Heptapleurum chanii, also known as Chan's fig, is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae, native to tropical forests of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Heptapleurum chanii include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Heptapleurum chanii is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
